message:
fix: bool() builtin properly raises TypeError for bad __bool__ returns

- Changed bool() codegen to use boolBuiltinCall() which returns !bool
  instead of toBool() which silently converts errors to false
- boolBuiltinCall now handles mutable __bool__ methods by casting away const
- genReturn now applies magic method conversion (validateBoolReturn) before
  self dereference, ensuring __bool__ return self properly validates type
- genBool now passes extra arguments to runtime for proper TypeError on
  too many arguments

Python 3 requires __bool__ to return exactly bool type. Previously:
- class Foo: def __bool__(self): return self  # would return false
Now properly raises TypeError as Python does.
